volumeTitle: Casina. The Casket Comedy. Curculio. Epidicus. The Two Menaechmuses
label: Epidicus (Section 5.1.615 - Section 5.2.678b)

sections.label: Section 5.1.615
sections.original: Quid est tibi ?
sections.translation: What ’ s the matter with you ?

sections.label: Section 5.1.615b
sections.original: Quin tu mi adornas ad fugam viaticum ,
sections.translation: Why don ’ t you prepare for me the necessaries for flight before I ’ m quite undone ? For the two fleeced old gentlemen are hunting for me through the city ; they are carrying in their hands handcuffs an inch and a half thick .

sections.label: Section 5.1.618
sections.original: Habe bonum animum .
sections.translation: Be of good heart —

sections.label: Section 5.1.618b
sections.original: Quippe ego quoi libertas in mundo sitast .
sections.translation: Of course I will , whose freedom is so close at hand [*] .

sections.label: Section 5.1.619
sections.original: Ego te servabo .
sections.translation: I will preserve you .

sections.label: Section 5.1.619b
sections.original: Edepol me illi melius , si nacti fuant .
sections.translation: I ’ faith , they ’ ll do it better for me , if they catch me .

sections.label: Section 5.1.620
sections.original: sed quis haec ést muliercula et ille gravastellus qui venit ?
sections.translation: But who ’ s this young woman , this greyish old fellow , too , that ’ s coming along ?

sections.label: Section 5.1.621
sections.original: Hic est danista , haec illa est autem , quam emi de praeda .
sections.translation: This is the Banker , and this is the woman whom I purchased out of the spoil .

sections.label: Section 5.1.621b
sections.original: Haecinest ?
sections.translation: What , is this she ?

sections.label: Section 5.1.622
sections.original: Haec est . estne ita ut tibi dixi ? aspecta et contempla , Epidice :
sections.translation: It is she . Isn ’ t she just like what I told you ? Look at her .

sections.label: Section 5.1.623
sections.original: usque ab unguiculo ad capillum summumst festivissuma .
sections.translation: Survey her , Epidicus . [*] Even from her nail to the top of her hair she is most lovely ! Is she not ? Do look at her ! Observe her ! You ’ ll be looking at a picture beautifully painted .

sections.label: Section 5.1.625
sections.original: Ex tuis verbis meum futurum corium pulchrum praedicas ,
sections.translation: Judging from your words you are foretelling that my hide will be beautifully painted ; me , whom Apelles and Zeuxis [*] , the pair of them , will be painting with elm-tree pigments .

sections.label: Section 5.1.627
sections.original: Di immortales , sicin iussi ad me ires ? pedibus † pulmunes
sections.translation: Immortal Gods ! I ’ m surprised at your slowness . The man that ’ s spoken of in the proverb with swollen feet , would have got here sooner than you have arrived for me .

sections.label: Section 5.1.629
sections.original: Haec edepol remorata med est .
sections.translation: I ’ faith , ’ twas she delayed me .

sections.label: Section 5.1.629b
sections.original: Siquidem istius gratia
sections.translation: If indeed you delayed for her sake ,

sections.label: Section 5.1.630
sections.original: id quod ista voluit , nimium advenisti cito .
sections.translation: because she wished it , you have come too quickly .

sections.label: Section 5.1.631
sections.original: Age age , absolve me atque argentum numera , ne comites morer .
sections.translation: Well , well , dispatch with me and count out the money , that I mayn ’ t be detaining my friends .

sections.label: Section 5.1.632
sections.original: Pernumeratumst .
sections.translation: It has been counted out .

sections.label: Section 5.1.632b
sections.original: Tene cruminam : huc inde .
sections.translation: Take this bag ; put it into it .

sections.label: Section 5.1.632c
sections.original: Sapienter venis .
sections.translation: You come discreetly provided ! Wait till I bring out the money to you .

sections.label: Section 5.1.633b
sections.original: Matura .
sections.translation: Make haste .

sections.label: Section 5.1.633c
sections.original: Domist .—
sections.translation: It ’ s at home .

sections.label: Section 5.1.634
sections.original: Satin ego oculis utilitatem optineo sincere an parum ?
sections.translation: Have I the use of my eyes quite unimpaired , or is it otherwise ?

sections.label: Section 5.1.635
sections.original: videon ego Telestidem te , Periphanai filiam ,
sections.translation: Do I not behold in you , Telestis , the daughter of Periphanes , born at Thebes of your mother Philippa , and conceived at Epidaurus ?

sections.label: Section 5.1.637
sections.original: Quis tu homo es , qui meum parentum nomen memoras et meum ?
sections.translation: What person are you who are making mention of the name of my parents and my own ?

sections.label: Section 5.1.638
sections.original: Non me nosti ?
sections.translation: Don ’ t you know me ?

sections.label: Section 5.1.638b
sections.original: Quod quidem nunc veniat in mentem mihi .
sections.translation: Not , indeed , so far as recurs to my mind just now .

sections.label: Section 5.1.639
sections.original: Non meministi me auream ad te afferre natali die
sections.translation: Don ’ t you remember my bringing you a crescent upon your birthday , and a little gold ring for your finger ?

sections.label: Section 5.1.640b
sections.original: Memini , mi homo .
sections.translation: I remember it . What , are you that person ?

sections.label: Section 5.1.641b
sections.original: Ego sum , ét istic frater , qui te mercatust , tuos .
sections.translation: I am , and ( pointing to STRATIPPOCLES , at a distance ) he there is your brother by another mother and the same father .

sections.label: Section 5.1.643
sections.original: Quid pater meus ? vivit ?
sections.translation: What of my father ? Is he alive ?

sections.label: Section 5.1.643b
sections.original: Animo liquido et tranquillo es , tace .
sections.translation: Be of calm and composed feelings ; hold your peace .

sections.label: Section 5.1.644
sections.original: Di me ex perdita servatam cupiunt , si vera autumas .
sections.translation: The Gods will that from being lost I should be saved , if you speak the truth .

sections.label: Section 5.1.645
sections.original: Non habeo ullam occasionem , ut apud te falsa fabuler .
sections.translation: I have no occasion to be telling untruths to you .

sections.label: Section 5.1.646
sections.original: Accipe argentum hoc , danista . hic sunt quadraginta minae .
sections.translation: Take this money , Banker ; here are forty minae . If any piece shall be doubtful I ’ ll change it .

sections.label: Section 5.1.647b
sections.original: Bene fecisti , bene vale .—
sections.translation: You do well . Kindly farewell .

sections.label: Section 5.1.648
sections.original: Nunc enim tú mea es .
sections.translation: Now then you are my own —

sections.label: Section 5.1.648b
sections.original: Soror quidem edepol , ut tu aeque scias .
sections.translation: Why yes — sister , i ’ faith , that you may know it as well . Greetings to you , brother .

sections.label: Section 5.1.649b
sections.original: Sanan haec est ?
sections.translation: Is this woman in her senses ?

sections.label: Section 5.1.649c
sections.original: Sana , si appellat suom .
sections.translation: In her senses , if she calls you her brother .

sections.label: Section 5.1.650
sections.original: Quid ? ego modo amator sum huic frater factus , dum intro eo atque exeo ?
sections.translation: How ’ s this ? Have I just now become her brother while going in-doors and coming out ?

sections.label: Section 5.1.651
sections.original: Quod boni est id tacitus taceas tute tecum et gaudeas .
sections.translation: What good fortune there is , do you in silence keep your peace thereon and rejoice .

sections.label: Section 5.1.652
sections.original: Perdidisti et repperisti me , soror .
sections.translation: Sister , you have both lost and found me !

sections.label: Section 5.1.652b
sections.original: , tace .
sections.translation: Simpleton , hold your tongue ! Through my endeavours , there ’ s ready for you at home , in fact , a Music-girl for you to make love to ; I too , through my endeavours , have restored your sister to liberty .

sections.label: Section 5.1.655
sections.original: Epidice , fateor —
sections.translation: Epidicus , I confess —

sections.label: Section 5.1.655b
sections.original: Abi intro ac iúbe huic aquam calefieri ;
sections.translation: Be off into the house , and order the water to be made warm [*] for her . The rest I ’ ll let you know afterwards , when there ’ s leisure .

sections.label: Section 5.1.657
sections.original: Sequere hac me , soror .
sections.translation: Follow me this way , sister .

sections.label: Section 5.1.657b
sections.original: Ego ad vos Thesprionem iussero
sections.translation: I ’ ll bid Thesprio [*] come across to you . But remember , if the old gentlemen are at all savage , you , with your sister , to run and help me .

sections.label: Section 5.1.659b
sections.original: Facile istuc erit .—
sections.translation: That will be easy .

sections.label: Section 5.1.660
sections.original: Thesprio , exi istac per hortum , adfer domum auxilium mihi ,
sections.translation: Thesprio , come this way through the garden . Come to my rescue at home ! The matter ’ s of importance ! ( To himself .) I care much less for the old fellows than I did just now . I ’ ll return in-doors , that the strangers may be attended to on their arrival . I ’ ll tell these same things that I know , in-doors to Stratippocles . I shall not take to flight ; I ’ m determined to be there at home , and he shan ’ t throw it in my teeth

sections.label: Section 5.1.665
sections.original: pedibus sese provocatum . abeo intro , nimis longum loquor .—
sections.translation: that he has been provoked by my running away [*] . I ’ ll away in-doors ; I ’ ve been talking too long .

sections.label: Section 5.2.666
sections.original: Satine illic homo ludibrio nos vetulos decrepitos duos
sections.translation: Hasn ’ t this fellow quite made a laughing-stock of us two decrepit old people [*] ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.667b
sections.original: Immo edepol tu quidem míserum med habes miseris modis .
sections.translation: Why yes , I ’ faith , you ’ ve really kept me plagued in a shocking fashion .

sections.label: Section 5.2.668
sections.original: Tace sis , modo sine me hóminem apisci .
sections.translation: Now do hold your tongue . Only let me catch the fellow !

sections.label: Section 5.2.668b
sections.original: Dico ego tibi iam , ut scias :
sections.translation: I ’ ll tell you now , that you may know it . It ’ s best for you to seek another companion ; so much , while I ’ ve been following you ,

sections.label: Section 5.2.670
sections.original: lassitudine invaserunt misero in genua flemina .
sections.translation: has the congested blood , from weariness , come down into the knees of poor me .

sections.label: Section 5.2.671
sections.original: Quot illic homo hodie me exemplis ludificatust atque te ,
sections.translation: After how many fashions has this fellow made sport of me and you to-day ! besides , how he has disembowelled my silver resources for me !

sections.label: Section 5.2.673
sections.original: Apage illum a me , nam ille quidem Volcani iratist filius :
sections.translation: Away with him from me ; for surely he ’ s the son of Vulcan in his wrath : wherever he touches , he sets all on fire ; if you stand by him , he scorches you with his heat .

sections.label: Section 5.2.675
sections.original: Duodecim deis plus quam in caelo deorumst immortalium
sections.translation: More than the twelve Gods , the immortal Gods as many as are in the heavens , are now assistants in my rescue , and are fighting on my side . As to whatever I have offended in , I have aid and assistance at home [*] . All my enemies I quite spurn by the heels [*] .

sections.label: Section 5.2.678b
sections.original: Vbi illum quaeram gentium ?
sections.translation: Where in the world shall I look for him ?
